entities.name: vāgrūpatā
entities.cite: the vag-rupata, the word-formedness, sasvati, standing, were utkramet

entities.name: prakāśa
entities.cite: the light would not illuminate — cognition would lose precisely what makes it *cognition of* something

entities.name: pratyavamarśa
entities.cite: the power of pratyavamarsa, that taking-as, that inner 'this is such-and-such', by which a bare impingement becomes an apprehension

axioms.name: sasvata
axioms.cite: the vag-rupata, the word-formedness, sasvati, standing
it, the word-form, is the recognizer, the power of pratyavamarsa

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: Light that reveals nothing determinate is not light; awareness stripped of the articulating thread would be a shining that shines on nothing, which is no shining at all

axioms.name: niyamaka
axioms.cite: to be aware is to be articulately aware, and articulation is the word's work

denials.name: svatantra
denials.cite: So the interweaving of 1.123 is not an empirical regularity that might have exceptions but a condition of the possibility of cognition as such

claims.cite: it is that which makes recognition possible

rejected_readings.label: prakasha_svatantra
rejected_readings.rendering: The light of consciousness shines independently, even without word-formedness or articulation.
rejected_readings.why: The verse explicitly denies this: prakasha cannot illuminate if vagrupa departs from avabodha. The commentary insists this is not empirical contingency but transcendental necessity — bare impingement without articulation is no cognition at all, hence no light.

rejected_readings.label: vagrupa_contingent_feature
rejected_readings.rendering: Word-formedness is merely a contingent feature that happens to accompany awareness but could be absent.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary makes this necessity explicit: 'to be aware is to be articulately aware, and articulation is the word's work' — vagrupa is not incidental but constitutive of avabodha itself. This reading treats the niyamaka relation as absent or optional, contradicting the axiom that vagrupa niyamaka avabodha.

rejected_readings.label: pratyavamarsa_mere_reflective_act
rejected_readings.rendering: Recognition (pratyavamarsa) is merely a reflective mental act that can occur independently of the fundamental word-power.
rejected_readings.why: Treats pratyavamarsa as a contingent operation of mind rather than identifying it with the essential word-formedness power itself. The identity vagrupa = pratyavamarsa forbids treating them as separable; this reading implicitly denies that identity by demoting pratyavamarsa to a secondary reflective operation.

notes: This verse establishes a transcendental condition: word-formedness (vagrupa), the power of articulation and determination, is not a contingent feature of awareness but its very ground. Without it, prakasha (illumination/cognition) cannot function — bare impingement collapses into non-cognition. The identity vagrupa = pratyavamarsa encodes that articulation IS the determining act of consciousness. The Vrtti's strong reading (extending word-association into sleep and indeterminate cognitions) is encoded here: no wordless stratum of awareness exists. The contested note flags the range from weak (all judgement is verbal) to strong (all sentience is verbal); the contract encodes the strong reading that became foundational to Kashmir Saiva Tantra.
